message:
Add beads issue tracker with 92 work items across all phases

Initialize the beads (br) issue tracker for project task management.
The .beads/ directory contains the git-syncable subset of the tracker
state; the SQLite database and ephemeral lock files are excluded by
the nested .gitignore.

Configuration:
- Issue prefix: "bd" (beads default)
- Export format: JSONL (one issue per line, machine-readable)

The issues.jsonl contains 92 structured work items covering the full
implementation plan:

- Phase 2 (storage layer): UnixVfs with asupersync I/O, pager with
  page cache and lock management, B-tree cell format parsing, B-tree
  cursor traversal, B-tree page splitting and balancing, B-tree
  property-based tests
- Phase 3 (WAL + crash recovery): MVCC-aware WAL frame format, crash
  model specification, checkpoint algorithm, recovery with injected
  partial writes and corruption
- Phase 4 (MVCC): core types and version store, page lock table,
  write coordinator, visibility predicate, garbage collection, buffer
  pool with ARC eviction
- Phase 5 (SQL pipeline): hand-written recursive descent parser with
  Pratt expression parsing, AST node definitions, query planner with
  index selection, VDBE bytecode compiler and interpreter with 190+
  opcodes
- Phase 6 (extensions): FTS5 inverted index, R-tree spatial indexing,
  JSON1 path queries, Session changesets, ICU collation
- Phase 7 (CLI shell): frankentui integration, dot-commands, output
  modes, REPL with history
- Phase 8 (conformance): SLT test harness, oracle comparison, full
  conformance suite targeting 95%+ parity
- Phase 9 (optimization): profile-driven hot path tuning, end-to-end
  integration tests

Each issue includes structured fields: id, title, description with
context/goal/acceptance-criteria, status, priority, dependencies
(blocks/parent-child relationships), and labels. The dependency graph
enforces correct implementation ordering across phases.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
